dynamic_line: Need medical attention?
I'm a bit busy with the wounded, but I can spare a moment.
This field hospital is overwhelmed. Too many casualties, not enough supplies.
Are you injured? I can take a look if you need help.

responses.text: Do you need any medical supplies?
I'll leave you to your work.

dynamic_line: Captain Richards, Royal Army Medical Corps. Been patching up soldiers for fifteen years.
Lieutenant Murray, RAMC. I was stationed at Camp Bastion before... all this.
Sergeant Cooper, combat medic. I've seen a lot of wounds, but nothing like what those things can do.
Staff Sergeant Williams, field medic. Just trying to keep people alive in this nightmare.

responses.text: How did you end up here?

dynamic_line: Was treating casualties when the evacuation order came. Couldn't leave my patients behind.
Our medical unit was deployed to assist with the crisis. Then it all went to hell.
I was on a training exercise with NATO forces when it happened. Been trying to save who I can ever since.
Field hospital was overrun. A few of us escaped with what supplies we could carry. Set up here to treat survivors.
The Hippocratic oath doesn't have an exception for the apocalypse. We do what we must.
I've had to make some difficult choices. Who to treat, who to... let go. It weighs on you.
Medicine in a crisis is about triage. Focus on those you can save. Still doesn't make it easier.
Thank you. But I'm just doing my job. Same as always, just with more zombies about.
Not good. Limited supplies, limited personnel. Doing what we can with what we have.
Some will make it, others... well, we're doing our best. Infection is our biggest concern now.
Stable for now. The ones who survived the initial attacks have a fighting chance if we can keep them supplied.
We've lost too many. But those still with us are fighters. British resolve and all that.

responses.text: What kind of injuries are you seeing?

dynamic_line: Lacerations, puncture wounds, blunt force trauma. Those things aren't subtle in their attacks.
Bite wounds are the worst. High risk of infection, and not just the normal kind.
Broken bones, internal bleeding, shock. Combat injuries plus whatever those creatures inflict.
Everything from minor scratches to... well, injuries incompatible with life. It's grim work.
Always. Antibiotics, painkillers, clean bandages. We're running critically low.
Desperately. Especially antiseptics and suture kits. Infection is a death sentence these days.
We need everything. First aid kits, surgical tools, IV fluids. Whatever you can find.
Yes. Our supply convoy never arrived. If you find any medical supplies, we'd put them to good use.

responses.text: I'll keep an eye out for medical supplies.

dynamic_line: That would be tremendously helpful. You'd be saving lives.
Thank you. Every bit helps when you're trying to keep people alive.
We'd be grateful for anything you can bring. These soldiers deserve a fighting chance.
Brilliant. Check pharmacies and hospitals if you can, but be careful. Those places are usually overrun.
